at_a_glance: You have spent $2.89 across 4 sessions in a single day. April 18 session 2 was the heaviest — $1.30 to build all of Phase 1 (9 tasks + review + parallel orchestration). Costs are healthy and trending down: the last two sessions averaged $0.49, well below the $0.90 session 2 peak.

session_narratives.2026-04-18-001: Kicked off the project with brainstorm, architecture, and spec-review — three planning meta-tasks that established the full spec and architecture before any code was written. Cost was $0.606, typical for a planning session of this depth.
session_narratives.2026-04-18-002: The biggest session — 11 tasks covering all of Phase 1 plus the phase review, using vibe-parallel for parallel subagent execution. At $1.30 it is the most expensive session, but it shipped the entire Electron foundation in one go.
session_narratives.2026-04-18-003: Spec and planning session for the F-STATE feature — vibe-add-feature generated FEATURE_TASKS.md and vibe-spec-review audited it. No code was committed; $0.47 was the cost of having a clean spec before building.
session_narratives.2026-04-18-004: Built all 5 F-STATE tasks (JS foundation, DOM, CSS, IPC, boot wiring) via a 3-wave parallel execution plan. At $0.513 and $0.103 per task, this is the most cost-efficient build session so far.

recommendations.title: CLAUDE.md active feature block breaks prompt cache
recommendations.body: CLAUDE.md embeds an Active Feature block (currently F-STATE) that is rewritten at the start of each new feature. This means the CLAUDE.md hash changes between features, resetting the cache. Moving the active feature context to a separate file (e.g. vibe/.active-feature.md) would keep CLAUDE.md stable and allow caching across an entire feature build phase.

recommendations.title: Wave 3 could have run parallel with Wave 2
recommendations.body: The F-STATE build used vibe-parallel with 3 waves: Wave 1 (FST-002 + FST-004 parallel), Wave 2 (FST-003 CSS), Wave 3 (FST-005 boot). FST-003 (CSS) and FST-005 (boot wiring) have no shared writes — they could have run in the same wave, saving one round-trip. Worth checking dependency graph before sequencing future feature waves.

forecast_narrative: Four features remain in Phase 2: F-FIRST-RUN, F-SPEECH, F-CLAUDE, and F-ACTIONS — each needing a spec session (~$0.45) and a build session (~$0.55), totalling roughly $4.00. Phase 3 polish (4 tasks) and two review gates add ~$0.60. Estimated project total at completion: $7.40-8.50. Fixing the CLAUDE.md caching issue before F-SPEECH could trim $0.40-0.80 off that range.
